zotero.abstractNote: We propose a novel pruning method for neural networks based on the Dirichlet distribution. By placing a Dirichlet prior on the weights of a layer, we can interpret the pruning process as a Bayesian model selection problem. We show that this approach leads to a simple and effective pruning criterion that can be applied to various network architectures. Our method is computationally efficient and does not require retraining, making it suitable for large-scale models. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several benchmarks, showing that it achieves competitive compression rates while maintaining high accuracy.
zotero.conferenceName: 24th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 24th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
